Petition And Declaration To Correct Clerical Errors To Spusal Property Orders
Off Calendar Nov-26-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Callahan for Snezana Pirslin. Gary Schwartz specially appearing for Brett Zebrowski. Seth Skootsky for and with Toma Pirslin (Beneficiary).

The Petition And Declaration To Correct Clerical Error To Spousal Property Orders was granted. Mr. Callahan will submit an amended Spousal Property Order on the required judicial counsel form. In regards to the 125 Rosecrans property, Mr. Callahan argued that the contract was not enforcable, that the appraised value was higher than the sale amount in the contract. Mr. Skootsky expressed that the contract was not filed as a creditor's claim before the time expired, and that there was no signature or initials of the decedent on the purchase contract. Mr. Schwartz presented a copy of the purchase contract signed by the decedent. The Court ruled that contract was entered by the decedent and that the Administrator should proceed to complete the sale. In regards to the 121 Rosecrans property, the parties agreed to go ahead with the contract with the exception of the payment of the commission to Brett Zebrowski and Palm Realty Boutique, which the Court granted. The issue regarding the commissions is continued to December 31, 2007. Mr. Callahan will file his supplement today. Mr. Schwartz is to file his brief by December 10, 2007. Mr. Callahan will file his response to the brief by December 17, 2007. The Court stated that the parties could decide to submit by briefs or to appear by phone. The Petition for Instructions was denied. Mr. Callahan is to prepare all the orders.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.
Bailiff: Deputy Lee.
